LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER GUY MOORE JR.'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED AS TO THE TRAVEL COSTS AND DENIED AS TO THE FILING FEES FOR THE ANSWER AND OPPOSITION TO THE WRIT. ROUTINE EXPENSES FOR LOCAL TRAVEL BY ATTORNEYS ARE NOT REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE LITIGATION. THE FILING FEES WERE NECESSARILY INCURRED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. calendar.matters:
Motion To Tax Costs , Index Of Non California Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM C. MCNEILL OF THE LEGAL AID SOCIETY, TEL. NO. 864-8848 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. BEVERLY R. MYERS OFFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5545 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. TARUN MEHTA OF LITTLER MENDELSON, TEL. NO. (925) 927-4513 APPEARING FOR THE REAL PARTY IN INTEREST GEO GROUP, INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER GUY MOORE JR.'S Motion To Tax Costs IS Granted as to the travel costs and denied as to the filing fees for the answer and opposition to the writ. Routine expenses for local travel by attorneys are not reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation. The filing fees were necessarily incurred. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: La Vena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/HK
